events.label: reasoning
events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"critical","work_stage":"in-progress","component_conditions":["non-compliant"],"percent_complete":40,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.9,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["HV insulating glove class incorrect for system voltage and/or test date expired per glove stamp","Use of expired or wrong-class PPE for live HV work violates OEM HV procedures and NFPA 70E-adjacent requirements"],"actions":["Stop all HV work immediately and de-energize/isolate the HV system per OEM lockout/tagout","Remove out-of-date/wrong-class gloves from service and quarantine","Obtain correct class HV gloves with current test date and verify class rating and dielectric test stamp before resuming","Re-verify all required HV PPE (gloves, leather protectors, face shield, arc-rated clothing) before any further HV exposure"],"rationale":"Wrong-class or expired HV gloves do not provide certified insulation for the system voltage; continuing live HV work with non-compliant PPE is an immediate electrocution/arc-flash hazard.","references":["OEM HV procedures","NFPA 70E-adjacent (PPE voltage rating and test interval requirements)"]}
